subject: Fw: Heli pad
body:
>
>Sarah, could you run this by JE, I spoke to George and Darren Rothell, YES you can land an S76 in grass, BUT Darren said usually you sink 6 inches into the ground and have to be carefull when takeing off due to Dynamic roll over, this happens when a skid or wheel gets stuck in the Mud and causes Helicopter to roll over on take off from an soft surface, the S76 weights 12,500 lbs compared to 4,500 lbs for Bell 407. 
>I think grass would be to soft for S76, LV
>Sent from my BlackBerry® wireless handheld
body_html:
Sarah, could you run this by JE, I spoke to George and Darren Rothell, YES you can land an S76 in grass, BUT Darren said usually you sink 6 inches into the ground and have to be carefull when takeing off due to Dynamic roll over, this happens when a skid or wheel gets stuck in the Mud and causes Helicopter to roll over on take off from an soft surface, the S76 weights 12,500 lbs compared to 4,500 lbs for Bell 407.
I think grass would be to soft for S76, LV
Sent from my BlackBerry® wireless handheld
